Orthographic variation (Suñiga vs. Zuñiga, Polonia vs. Apolonia) is acceptable and reflects period spelling inconsistency documented in the sources themselves. Corroboration: two independent original parish registers are cited—Adrian's 1913 marriage record (informant: Adrian himself) and Ramon's 1887 baptism (informant: presenting parent(s) circa 1887)—separated by 26 years and different informant circumstances, both naming the same parents. The compound-surname structure provides additional independent structural corroboration. Tier: the declaration of 'proved' is justified—direct evidence from an original parish register (the 1913 marriage) plus independent corroborating baptism record, no contradicting evidence, exhaustive search documented. entry.question: What does the baptismal record for Adrián Zuñiga Rojas, born 8 September 1889 in Arani, Cochabamba, Bolivia, record about his parents?
entry.rationale: Catholic parish baptismal registers are the primary vital records for late-19th-century Bolivia. Arani, Cochabamba was an established parish town; records for 1889 are likely to survive and to name the godparents and biological parents. This is the gatekeeper question — finding the baptismal entry would directly answer the objective and determine whether additional corroboration questions are needed.

entry.rationale: Bolivia, Baptisms, 1560–1938 (collection 1520550) is the primary indexed source for Catholic baptisms in Bolivia. Arani parish (San Bartolomé) records are included; volumes show 93–100% indexed coverage. The baptismal entry for Adrián Zuñiga Rojas on or around 8 September 1889 would name both parents by their full compound surnames — directly answering the research question.

Bolivia, Catholic Church Records, 1566–2020 (collection 1922463) is a broader collection (9.8M records vs 1.87M in the Baptisms-only set) that may contain images or index entries not captured in the dedicated baptisms collection. Search this if pli_001 yields no result — it is the most complete Bolivia church record set on FamilySearch.

Multiple browse-only image groups for San Bartolomé, Arani parish (imageGroupPrefixes 005091539, 005091540, 005091541) exist on FamilySearch with no explicit date range shown in the volume metadata. If the indexed searches (pli_001, pli_002) fail to surface the 1889 baptism, direct image browsing of these volumes may locate the entry. All volumes are 93–100% indexed, but index gaps could cause the record to be missed in keyword search.

Bolivia, Marriages, 1630–1940 (collection 1520564) — searching for the marriage of Adrián's parents (a man surnamed Zuñiga marrying a woman surnamed Rojas in or near Arani, probably 1865–1892). A marriage record would corroborate the parentage found in the baptism and may supply additional identifying details (parents' birthplaces, ages, or their own parents). Also serves as an independent line of evidence in case the baptism is missing or ambiguous.

Ancestry Bolivia Select Baptisms, 1560–1938 (https://www.ancestry.com/search/collections/9795/) indexes the same underlying records as the FamilySearch baptisms collection but uses an independent indexing and name-matching algorithm. Searching Ancestry separately accounts for name-variant matching differences and any records indexed by Ancestry but not by FamilySearch. Use if pli_001 and pli_002 are unproductive.

FAN pivot: siblings of Adrián Zuñiga Rojas baptized in Arani before and after 1889 would share the same parents and corroborate who they were. Searching Bolivia, Baptisms (collection 1520550) for other children with surname Zuñiga Rojas (or Zuñiga + Rojas) in Arani from roughly 1870–1892 tests whether additional records independently name the same father and mother — strengthening the proof argument and potentially surfacing variant spellings.

entry.narrative_markdown:
## The Parents of Adrián Zuñiga Rojas of Arani, Cochabamba, Bolivia

### Conclusion

The evidence establishes that **Adrián Zuñiga Rojas** (born 8 September 1889, Arani, Cochabamba, Bolivia; died 21 April 1928, Arani) was the son of **Pedro Suñiga (Zuñiga)** and **Polonia Rojas**, both of Arani, Cochabamba, Bolivia.

### Primary Evidence: Adrián's 1913 Marriage Record

The strongest and most direct evidence is Adrián's own Catholic marriage record at San Bartolomé parish, Arani, dated 29 November 1913.¹ In this record the groom is identified as "Adrián Suñiga" (born 1889), and both parents are explicitly named: his father as **Pedro Suñiga** and his mother as **Polonia Rojas**. The bride is Gregoria Peñarrieta (born 1893), daughter of Ciriaco Peñarrieta and Adriana Obando.

This record is an original Catholic parish register (source classification: *original*). The information regarding the parents' names is primary — Adrián, as an adult participant at his own marriage, self-reported his parentage to the recording priest. The evidence is direct: the record explicitly names Pedro Suñiga and Polonia Rojas in the father-of-groom and mother-of-groom roles (assertions a_004–a_007). There is no known incentive for Adrián to misrepresent his parents at his own marriage ceremony.

All four parentage assertions from this record share a single informant (Adrián himself) and therefore constitute one evidential unit per GPS Standard 46 — they receive no more combined weight than the strongest single item.

### Corroborating Evidence: 1887 Baptism of Ramón Suñiga Rojas

Independent corroboration comes from a Catholic baptism record at San Bartolomé parish, Arani, dated 30 August 1887 — twenty-six years before the marriage record and two years before Adrián's birth.² This record documents the baptism of **Ramón Suñiga Rojas**, a male child, naming his father as **Pedro Suñiga** and his mother as **Polonia Rojas** (assertions a_014–a_017).

This source is also an original Catholic parish register. The information is primary — the parent(s) presenting Ramón for baptism reported both parents' names to the priest. The evidence is indirect with respect to Adrián's parentage, requiring the inference that Ramón was Adrián's sibling sharing the same parents. That inference is supported by three converging lines: (1) the same parish and overlapping time period, (2) the compound surname of the baptized child, and (3) Adrián's own compound surname encoding the same parentage (discussed below).

These two records are genuinely independent informant units. The 1913 informant is Adrián himself at his marriage; the 1887 informant is the presenting parent(s) at Ramón's baptism two and a half decades earlier. Different event, different year, different informant circumstances.

### Bolivian Compound-Surname Convention as Corroborating Structure

Under the Catholic Bolivian naming convention of the late nineteenth century, a child's compound surname consists of the father's first (paternal) surname followed by the mother's first (paternal) surname. The name "Adrián Zuñiga Rojas" (or "Suñiga Rojas" in the spelling variant prevalent in Arani records) directly encodes the parentage: Zuñiga/Suñiga from the father, Rojas from the mother. Ramón's full compound surname "Suñiga Rojas" in his 1887 baptism record reflects precisely the same structure. Both the marriage record and the baptism record are therefore internally consistent with each other and with the naming convention.

### Additional Siblings (Supporting Context)

A FAN search of FamilySearch collection 1922463 identified additional apparent children of Pedro Zuñiga/Suñiga × Polonia Rojas recorded at San Bartolomé and Arani: Leonidas Suñiga (born and died 1886, San Bartolomé), Benita Zuñiga (born ~1895, San Bartolomé; died 1921), and Cecilia Zúñiga (born ~1889–1890, Arani). These records were not formally extracted into the evidence base but are consistent with the identified parents and the same parish.

### Negative Evidence and Research Limitations

Adrián's own baptism record was not found in any searched indexed collection. Bolivia Baptisms (FamilySearch collection 1520550) and the broader Bolivia Catholic Church Records (collection 1922463) were both searched without locating a baptism entry for Adrián in 1889 or nearby years. This absence likely reflects an indexing gap rather than the non-existence of the record: browse-only image volumes for San Bartolomé parish exist on FamilySearch (imageGroupPrefixes 005091539–005091541, approximately 1,323 images across six volumes) but require manual browsing, which was not possible in this research session. Locating Adrián's own baptism entry remains a recommended follow-up action.

No marriage record for Pedro Zuñiga × Polonia Rojas was found in Bolivia Marriages (collection 1520564) after four variant searches. This absence does not contradict the parentage conclusion; it may reflect indexing gaps for Arani parish in the relevant period.

No evidence naming different parents for Adrián was found in any of the ten searched and documented sources.

### Summary

Two independent original Catholic parish registers from San Bartolomé, Arani, Cochabamba, Bolivia — Adrián's 1913 marriage record and his sibling Ramón's 1887 baptism — both name **Pedro Suñiga (Zuñiga)** and **Polonia Rojas** as the parents of a Suñiga Rojas child from Arani. The compound-surname structure of Adrián's own name is consistent with this parentage. No contradicting evidence was found across ten logged searches. Research was declared reasonably exhaustive.

Documents prove and verify that **Adrián Zuñiga Rojas**, born 8 September 1889 in Arani, Cochabamba, Bolivia, was the son of **Pedro Suñiga (Zuñiga)** and **Polonia Rojas** of Arani, Cochabamba, Bolivia.
